content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

January 10, 1991

Heidy Foster  
District Representative  
Office of Senator Dan McCorquodale  
1020 15th Street, Suite B  
Modesto, CA 95354

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-739

Dear Ms. Foster:

We have received your letter dated December 5, 1990, seeking written confirmation of the telephone advice I provided to you regarding the limitations and reporting requirements for gifts under the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1

Your letter accurately summarizes the telephone advice that I provided to you. Money paid to you by the Hospital Council of Northern and Central California for travel expenses to attend a conference held by the council is considered a gift to you. (Section 82028.) The conference itself falls under the "informational material" exception of the Act's definition of gift. (Id.) Therefore any conference fee paid by the council is not a gift.

As a designated employee under the Senate's conflict-of-interest code, any gifts you receive aggregating $50.00 or more from a single source must be reported pursuant to the Senate's conflict-of-interest code. Prior to January 1, 1991, there was no limitation on the amount a legislative staff member could accept as gifts from nonlobbyists.2

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 The limits on gifts from lobbyists and lobbying firms is set forth at Section 86203.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Mark T. Morodomi
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:MTM:dg
Enclosures
